description: Islamic jurisprudence — prayer, fasting, zakat, hajj, purity, halal/haram, family law

questions.question: How many rak'ahs are in each of the five daily prayers? List all five.
What are the conditions (shurut) that make wudu (ablution) valid? What nullifies it?
What is the nisab for zakat on gold, silver, and money? What is the rate?
When does fasting in Ramadan become obligatory? List those who are exempt.
What are the pillars (arkan) of Hajj? Distinguish from wajibat.
What are the conditions for a valid Islamic marriage (nikah)?
What foods are haram in Islam? Cite Quran and Hadith.
What is the ruling on riba (interest/usury) in Islam? Cite the Quranic verse.
Explain the different types of divorce (talaq) in Islam.
What are the obligatory acts (fara'id) of ghusl (ritual bath)?
What are the conditions for zakah on agricultural produce (zakat al-zuru')? What is the rate?
What breaks the fast (what are the muftirat of Ramadan fasting)?
How is the Islamic will (wasiyyah) different from inheritance (miraath)? What are the shares in miraath?
What is the ruling on music and musical instruments in Islam? What do the four madhabs say?
Explain the concept of halal slaughter (dhabihah). What are its conditions?
What are the conditions for Friday prayer (Salat al-Jumu'ah) to be obligatory?
What is fidya and kaffarah? When are they required?
What are the Islamic rules for purification from najasah (ritual impurity)?
Is it permissible to pray behind an imam of a different madhab?
What is the Islamic ruling on combining (jam') and shortening (qasr) prayers during travel?
